symbols: Christ rising triumphantly — victory over death and the grave
Red banner/flag — the standard of the Resurrection, the victory of the Lamb
White loincloth — the burial garments now transformed into garments of glory
Fallen soldiers below — earthly power rendered helpless before divine resurrection
Landscape with town — the world into which the risen Christ returns
description: Christ rises dynamically into the air, nearly nude except for a white cloth around his waist, holding a tall staff with a red banner billowing behind him. His muscular body is brightly illuminated. His arms are spread wide in a gesture of triumph. Below him, two armored soldiers recoil and fall back in astonishment. A landscape with a distant church tower stretches behind. The composition is tall and vertical, emphasizing Christ's upward movement against a dramatic clouded sky.
theological_description: The Resurrection of Christ is the cornerstone of the Christian faith. Paul declares: 'If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain and your faith is in vain' (1CO.15.14). Christ's dynamic rising here conveys the explosive power of the event — not a gentle awakening but a cosmic triumph. The banner of the cross-standard proclaims victory: 'Death is swallowed up in victory' (1CO.15.54). The fallen soldiers recall Matthew's account: the guards 'shook and became like dead men' (MAT.28.4). Christ's glorified body — the same body that was crucified, now transformed — anticipates the promise to all believers: 'He will transform our lowly body to be like his glorious body' (PHP.3.21). The Resurrection fulfills Christ's own prophecy: 'Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up' (JHN.2.19), and vindicates the Father's acceptance of the Son's sacrifice.

scripture_refs.reason: The Resurrection account: the angel announces He is risen, the guards are overcome
The guards trembled and became like dead men — the fallen soldiers depicted
Death is swallowed up in victory — the triumphant energy of the risen Christ
Christ will transform our lowly body to be like His glorious body — the glorified risen form
Destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up — Christ's prophecy of His resurrection
